sections.heading: RT
sections.content:
Radiation therapy serves multiple roles in melanoma management including definitive treatment of in situ melanoma in medically inoperable patients, adjuvant therapy for high-risk primary tumors (desmoplastic/neurotropic melanoma), adjuvant nodal basin radiation for selected high-risk patients after therapeutic dissection, palliative treatment of symptomatic metastases, and definitive/stereotactic treatment of brain metastases [21], [26].

Principles:
- Adjuvant nodal external beam RT should be delivered using a technique judged optimal by the treating radiation oncologist. IMRT may lower toxicity and should be considered [21].
- Image-guided RT (IGRT) should be used where clinically appropriate [21].
- Hypofractionated regimens may increase risk for long-term complications [21].
- BRAF and/or MEK inhibitors may interact with radiation and can lead to increased toxicity. Consider holding BRAF/MEK inhibitors ≥3 days before and after fractionated RT and ≥1 day before and after SRS [42].
- Limited data suggest no clear evidence of consistent adverse interactions between immunotherapy and RT, though abscopal responses have been observed [42], [43].
- Adjuvant WBRT is NOT recommended after resection or SRS for melanoma brain metastases due to worse cognitive decline without OS benefit [26].

Dose Frameworks:
- Primary Disease - Definitive Therapy for MIS/LM: total dose 32-70 Gy, dose per fraction varies (2-8 Gy), fractions vary (5-35 fractions), schedule over 1-7 weeks. Indication: Definitive treatment for MIS/LM-type in medically inoperable patients or when surgical morbidity prohibitive. Regimens include: 64-70 Gy in 32-35 fractions over 6-7 weeks; 50-57.5 Gy in 20-28 fractions over 4-5 weeks; 35 Gy in 5 fractions over 1 week for fields <3 cm; 32 Gy in 4 fractions once per week [21].
- Adjuvant RT for Primary Site - Desmoplastic Melanoma: total dose 48-66 Gy, dose per fraction 2-3 Gy, fractions 20-33, schedule over 4-7 weeks. Indication: Select cases of high-risk desmoplastic melanoma based on risk factors for local recurrence (category 2B). Risk factors include T4 melanomas on head/neck with neurotropism, desmoplastic histology, close margins [21].
- Adjuvant RT for Nodal Basin: total dose 48-66 Gy, dose per fraction 2-3 Gy, fractions 20-33, schedule over 4-7 weeks. Indication: Selected patients at high risk for nodal recurrence based on non-response/non-receipt of neoadjuvant therapy and/or extracapsular extension, location, number, and size of involved nodes (category 2B) [21].
- SRS for Brain Metastases - Primary Treatment: total dose 15-24 Gy single fraction, dose per fraction 15-24 Gy, fractions 1, schedule single session. Indication: Smaller brain tumors: ≤20 mm max diameter receive up to 24 Gy; 21-30 mm receive up to 18 Gy; 31-40 mm receive up to 15 Gy. Caution for lesions >3 cm; single-fraction not typically recommended for >4 cm [26].
- SRS for Brain Metastases - Adjuvant After Resection: total dose 12-20 Gy single fraction, dose per fraction 12-20 Gy, fractions 1, schedule single session. Indication: Smaller cavities: <4.2 cc receive 20 Gy; 4.2 to <8.0 cc receive 18 Gy; 8.0 to <14.4 cc receive 17 Gy; 14.4 to <20 cc receive 15 Gy; 20 to <30 cc receive 14 Gy; 30 cc to <5 cm receive 12 Gy. Not recommended for cavities >5 cm [26].
- Palliative WBRT: total dose 20-30 Gy, dose per fraction 2-3 Gy, fractions 5-10, schedule over 1-2 weeks. Indication: Palliative purposes only when SRS/SRT not feasible. Consider HA-WBRT + memantine for better prognosis patients (≥4 months expected survival, no metastases within 5 mm of hippocampi). Standard WBRT 20 Gy in 5 fractions for poor prognosis [26].

Approaches:
- Adjuvant RT for Desmoplastic Neurotropic Melanoma: dose fractionation 48-66 Gy in 20-33 fractions over 4-7 weeks, concurrent chemotherapy none, indication select patients with high-risk desmoplastic melanoma, key trials retrospective studies by Strom et al., Guadagnolo et al. support improved local control, toxicities skin toxicity, fibrosis, lymphedema.
- Adjuvant RT for Nodal Basin Relapse Prevention: dose fractionation 48 Gy in 20 fractions over 4 weeks or other regimens, concurrent chemotherapy none, indication selected patients at high risk for nodal basin relapse, benefits weighed against toxicities including lymphedema.

timeline.title: Regimen
timeline.body: Fractionation varies widely: 5-35 fractions, dose range 15-70 Gy. Common adjuvant regimen: 48-66 Gy in 20-33 fractions over 4-7 weeks. SRS single fraction 15-24 Gy. Palliative WBRT 20-30 Gy in 5-10 fractions.
